focus: RQ3 deepening — model-profile schema + escalation matrix

description: Full JSON schema for model-profile.json with 8 model profiles (SWE-1.6, DeepSeek-v4-pro, Kimi-k2.6, Qwen3.6, GLM-5.1, gpt-5.5, claude-opus-4.7, claude-sonnet-4.7). Adapts smallcode ModelProfile struct (12 fields: context_length, tool_format, template, strengths, weaknesses, stop_sequences) with OpenCode extensions (id, provider, free_tier, pro_tier, escalation_target, average_iter_wall_clock_min). Includes JSON Schema validation and field descriptions.

source: iter-003.md pattern 5 (escalation provider config + canEscalate gate)
description: Concrete escalation decision matrix with IF/THEN rules for downgrade (4 rules), escalate (6 rules), quota-aware (5 rules), session gates (max 5 escalations, 10 downgrades, 5-min cooldown), and conversation format conversion (OpenAI <-> Anthropic). Rules reference model-profile.json escalation_target field for automatic escalation chain following. Priority order: session gates → quota-aware → escalate → downgrade.

source: sk-prompt assets structure + cli_prompt_quality_card.md mirror sync
description: Registry location verdict: Option A (.opencode/skills/sk-prompt/assets/model-profile.json). Rationale: (1) sk-prompt is cross-CLI master for prompt quality guidance (evidenced by cli_prompt_quality_card.md mirror sync), (2) model profiles inform prompt construction decisions (framework selection, pre-planning density), (3) avoids duplication vs top-level config, (4) consistent with existing sk-prompt/assets patterns, (5) aligns with sk-prompt's cross-cutting mission. Implementation includes mirror sync to cli-devin, cli-opencode, cli-codex, cli-gemini, cli-claude-code.

source: iter-003.md pattern 3 (Bayesian tool scoring) + mcp-code-mode SKILL.md
target: cli-* iter recipes per-call (NOT mcp-code-mode tool-registry)
description: Bayesian tool-scoring placement verdict: cli-* iter recipes per-call, NOT mcp-code-mode tool-registry layer. Rationale: (1) per-iteration model selection is CLI orchestrator concern, (2) mcp-code-mode is for external API integration not internal routing, (3) historical state belongs in skill tree (e.g., cli-devin/state/tool_scores.json), (4) per-call flexibility allows CLI-specific tuning, (5) Laplace smoothing formula is simple enough to inline, (6) tool demotion thresholds are CLI-specific. Each CLI skill implements its own scoring state, bayesian_scoring() helper ((successes+1)/(total+2)), and should_demote() helper (total_calls>=3 AND confidence<0.35).
